content.rendered:
On this page, you can find an overview of all articles related to the conjugation of verbs.

The Conjugation of Verbs

FINNISH TENSES AND MOODS

1. Present tense

    • The Finnish verbtypes

2. The past tenses

    • The imperfect tense
        • Lentää > lensin
        • Auttaa > autoin
    • The negative imperfect
    • The perfect tense
    • The plusquamperfect
    • Overview of past tenses

3. The future tense

  • Ways to express intent

4. The conditional mood

    • The conditional
    • The perfect conditional
    • The passive conditional

5. The imperative mood

    • The imperative
    • Älä! Negative imperative
    • Example sentences (singular)
    • Example sentences (plural)
    • Other imperative uses

6. The potential mood

    • The potential mood
Other verb forms

OTHER VERB FORMS

1. The passive forms

    • The present passive
    • The negative present passive
    • The passive imperfect
    • The passive perfect tense
    • The passive conditional
    • Overview of the passives
    • Spoken language passive

2. Consonant gradation

    • Verbtype 1 consonant gradation
    • Verbtype 3 consonant gradation
    • Verbtype 4 consonant gradation
    • Verbtype 6 consonant gradation

3. The infinitives

    • The 5 Finnish infinitives
    • The first infinitive
    • The second infinitive
    • The third infinitive
    • The fourth infinitive

4. The participles

    • Overview
    • Active present participle (-VA)
    • Passive present participle (-TAVA)
    • Active past participle (-NUT)
    • Passive past participle (-TU)
    • Agent participle (-mA)
More verb related topics

MORE VERB RELATED TOPICS

  • Verbtype 1 – Conjugation in all tenses and moods
  • Making verbs negative – full overview of all tenses and moods
  • Conjugation of verbs in spoken language
  • Finnish verb stems
  • The -matta/mättä form
  • A verbtype 2 peculiarity: Minä tupakoin ∼ tupakoitsen
